business.headline: IRDAI revamps key advisory committee, brings in industry and policy expertsThe reconstituted panel includes several key institutional representatives. These include members from the Life Insurance Council and the General Insurance Council, along with heads of important professional bodies.
Sensex ends 568 points higher, Nifty above 23,500; Eternal up 6%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 567.99 points to close at 76,070.84, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 172.35 points to end at 23,581.15.

LPG situation concerning: Government calls for judicious use amid shortage fearsThe Centre has urged judicious LPG use, pushed PNG adoption for commercial users, and asked states to fast-track approvals for city gas pipelines to ensure stable supply and avoid disruptions.

Did your health insurance premium jump at renewal? Here's what's behind itMany people are seeing sudden jumps in their health insurance premiums at renewal. The reasons aren't always clear, and insurers don't always explain them. In this article, we break down the possible reasons behind these sudden premium jumps.

From ITR revision to office commute relief: 5 big tax changes from April 1Starting April 1, several tax rules are set to change in ways that could directly impact your filing and savings. From ITR revisions to commute benefits, here's a quick look at what's new.

GSP Crop Science IPO: should you subscribe to the public listing? Check GMPGSP Crop Science IPO is a book-built issue of Rs 400.00 crore. It includes a fresh issue of 75 lakh shares worth Rs 240.00 crore and an offer for sale of 50 lakh shares worth Rs 160.00 crore.

Should married couples be allowed to file taxes together in India?In most homes, finances are shared. But when it comes to taxes, couples are treated as individuals. Should that change? A new idea is now putting this question in focus.

How goal-based investing can transform savers into long-term wealth-builders?Goal-based investing reframes personal finance from a pursuit of returns into a pursuit of readiness. The most meaningful financial success is not the highest number on a statement, but the confidence that money will be available when life demands it.

Power stocks rise today; Adani Power gains 2% after 1,600 MW deal winThe buying interest was visible across the sector, led by stocks such as Tata Power, NLC India, Torrent Power and Adani Power. Among the top movers, Adani Power remained in focus after a fresh business development.

West Asia conflict triggers global helium shortage. Why it's no laughing matterCrude oil and LNG have been in the limelight amid the ongoing West Asia war. But another critical gas, helium, is now facing a global supply shock and here's why it's bad news.
Sensex, Nifty open flat as crude oil prices weigh in on investor sentimentThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 63.36 points to 75,566.21, while the NSE Nifty50 added 21.90 points to 23,430.70 as of 9:42 am.

Explained: Why gold prices are not rising despite ongoing Iran warIt is unusual for gold to fall during volatile situations like war. But that is exactly what is happening now and it raises a key question about what is driving prices today

Tenant vs landlord: How India's new rental rules change the balanceRenting a home in India has long been a game of negotiation, where rules were often unclear and power rarely equal between tenants and landlords. A new rental framework based on the Model Tenancy Act, 2021 is trying to formalise how homes are rented in India.

Highlights: Govt monitors fuel, LPG supply as 27 states and UTs set up control roomsLPG crisis Highlights: Amid shortage fears triggered by ongoing conflict in West Asia, fresh supplies are beginning to arrive in India. After the Shivalik vessel reached on Monday, another LPG carrier, Nanda Devi, has now arrived at Gujarat's Vadinar port with over 47,000 metric tonnes, easing immediate pressure. Even so, the impact of earlier disruptions continues to be felt across sectors, with cities like Pune reporting strain and limited cafeteria operations. Authorities have also intensified action against hoarding, with raids, FIRs and arrests reported across states, while the government pushes commercial users to shift to piped natural gas and maintains there is no nationwide shortage.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early signals suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,515.50 at around 8:00 am. This points to the Nifty 50 opening above Monday's closing level of 23,408.8.

How young professionals are piling up Rs 10–50 lakh debt through small EMIsIt begins with a small EMI—a new phone, a quick getaway, but for many young professionals, these tiny payments are quietly adding up to tens of lakhs. Are they prepared to handle it?

Gold drops over Rs 3,400, silver cheaper by Rs 11,000: Is it time to invest?Despite global tensions, gold and silver turned cheaper, with gold down Rs 3,400 and silver down Rs 11,000. Does this decline signal a good entry point for investors?

Government urges LPG users to switch to PNG as companies roll out incentivesSujata Sharma, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, said the move has been discussed over the past few days as authorities look to ensure stable fuel availability across the country.
Sensex ends 939 points higher, Nifty above 23,300; SBI up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 938.93 points to end at 75,502.85, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 257.70 points to close at 23,408.80.

Saudi Arabia offers alternate Red Sea route as Strait of Hormuz faces crisisSaudi Arabia is now giving its long-term customers the option to receive their April oil allocations through the Red Sea port of Yanbu instead of the usual Persian Gulf terminals.

entertainment.headline: Sara Ali Khan asked to file affidavit for Kedarnath, Badrinath visit The Badri-Kedar Temple Committee has introduced a new rule requiring non-Hindu visitors to submit an affidavit affirming belief in Hinduism to offer prayers at Badrinath and Kedarnath. The directive applies to all non-Hindu devotees, including actor Sara Ali Khan, a frequent visitor to Kedarnath.

Academy 'extremely upset' after Teyana Taylor calls out security at Oscars 2026 The Academy issued a statement after Teyana Taylor reported being manhandled by a security guard at the Oscars. Both the Academy and the security firm addressed the incident and promised to improve guest experience.

Dune: Part Three teaser trailer out, shows a darker and intense story The teaser trailer for Dune: Part Three was unveiled in Los Angeles, offering a first look at the film's key characters and its high-stakes narrative. Directed by Denis Villeneuve, the film follows Paul Atreides as he grapples with the consequences of power.

Inside Alia Bhatt's fun-filled 33rd birthday at Disneyland, actor shares dreamy pics Actor Alia Bhatt marked her 33rd birthday with a social media post, giving fans a look at private moments from her celebrations in Hong Kong with husband Ranbir Kapoor and their daughter Raha.

Ghost War trailer unveils new mission for John Krasinski Jack Ryan Jack Ryan: Ghost War brings back John Krasinski and main cast members from the Prime Video series for a new film installment, set to premiere on 20 May. Directed by Andrew Bernstein, the film continues the story of Tom Clancy's iconic CIA analyst.

Jane Fonda questions Barbra Streisand's Robert Redford Oscar tribute Jane Fonda questioned Barbra Streisand's Oscar tribute to late actor Robert Redford. Fonda said that she worked with Redford in four films, while Streisand only made one.

Radhikka Madan on Irrfan: He empowered me as an artist and woman Radhikka Madan recalled working with Irrfan in Angrezi Medium. She said he helped her feel confident and treated her as an equal on set.

Spider-Man Brand New Day: First look out; check out trailer release date The first footage of Tom Holland in Spider-Man: Brand New Day has been released. Directed by Destin Daniel Cretton, the film will hit theatres on July 31.

Oscar winner Octavia Spencer on crime against women, Lost Women of Alaska series Octavia Spencer discusses reframing true crime in Lost Women of Alaska, shifting the focus from perpetrators to the resilience of indigenous survivors while interrogating systemic neglect and racism.

Dhurandhar 2 gets A certificate, run time revealed Just two days ahead of its highly anticipated release, Dhurandhar: The Revenge has been granted an A certificate. The makers have also announced the film's final runtime.

Hansika Motwani visits Golden Temple after divorce, shares hopeful note Hansika Motwani visited the Golden Temple in Amritsar, sharing photos and a cryptic message reflecting optimism and resilience. She recently got divorced from businessman Sohael Khaturiya.

Rashmika Mandanna resumes Mysaa shoot after wedding; shares on-set glimpse Rashmika Mandanna resumed the shooting of Mysaa, following her wedding celebrations. She shared an on-set glimpse from the upcoming action-thriller.

Viral: Kylie Jenner's awkward moment with Pauline Chalamet at Oscars goes viral A brief encounter between Kylie Jenner and Pauline Chalamet at the 98th Academy Awards has become a focal point of discussion on social media, with viewers and experts weighing in on the perceived awkwardness of the exchange.

Sarke Chunar: Lyricist says he was only asked to translate original Kannada song Lyricist Raqueeb Alam said he did not write the controversial Sarke Chunar song but had only translated the Kannada version despite having objections.

Kangana Ranaut on Nora Fatehi song: Bollywood has crossed all limits of vulgarity Kangana Ranaut has criticised Bollywood for its increasing vulgarity following the controversy over Nora Fatehi's song from KD – The Devil. She emphasised the need for stricter regulations to curb obscene content in films and music videos.

Silambarasaran shares BTS still from Arasan shoot, second schedule underway Actor Silambarasan is back on the sets of Arasan, with the second shooting schedule progressing fast in Chennai.  Fans are eagerly waiting for the film, which is part of Vetri Maaran's Vada Chennai universe.

Nora Fatehi song Sarke Chunar row: Cine worker body seeks action from I&B, censors All Indian Cine Workers Association has called on the CBFC and the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting to take strict action against the song Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke. In their letter, they cited public outrage and negative impact on youth.

Ali Fazal marks anniversary with sweet message for Richa Chadha: The rest is served Ali Fazal shared a sweet anniversary note for Richa Chadha along with candid pictures from their journey together. He called the day special and memorable.

Diljit Dosanjh's Main Vaapas Aaunga teaser in theatres with Dhurandhar 2? Imtiaz Ali hinted at the teaser for Main Vaapas Aaunga, featuring Diljit Dosanjh, being attached with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

general news.headline: Good thing: Trump reacts to counterterror chief's resignation over Iran warThe US president's remarks came hours after Joe Kent publicly announced he was stepping down because he opposed the Trump administration's military campaign in Iran, saying it was done due to pressure from Israel.

American mercenary Matthew VanDyke, 6 Ukrainians arrested in India by NIASix Ukrainians and a US citizen have been arrested by the NIA for entering restricted areas in Mizoram, crossing into Myanmar, and engaging with insurgent-linked groups. The anti-terror agency has alleged that the group had plans to train militants and supply weapons, including drones, in a suspected cross-border terror network.

Pak sleepwalked into Middle East maze, and 400 Afghans just paid for itAfter Pakistan signed the Strategic Mutual Defence Agreement with Saudi Arabia, it was obligated to help Riyadh amid Iranian drone and missile strikes in the Middle East. When Saudi Arabia needed military help and assurances, Pakistan carried out intensive bombing in Afghanistan to signal that it was already caught up in a war.

US started war due to pressure from Israel: Trump's counterterrorism chief quitsJoe Kent, in a post on social media, argued that Tehran did not pose an immediate threat to the US and questioned the reasons behind the war.

Video: Massive fire chokes Manhattan, thick smoke encompasses skylineThe fire started shortly before 10 am in a skyscraper near East 43rd Street and Madison Avenue, close to the busy area around Grand Central Terminal in New York City.

Don't need your assistance; never did: Trump to Nato after Iran war pushbackUS President Donald Trump said most NATO allies and partners have declined to join US operations in Iran or protect the Strait of Hormuz, despite supporting US objectives, raising concerns over energy security and pushing for diplomatic solutions.

PM speaks with UAE President, stresses safe passage through Strait of HormuzPM Modi strongly condemned recent attacks targeting the United Arab Emirates that led to the loss of innocent lives and damage to civilian infrastructure. He reiterated India's firm stance against such acts of violence.

Who is Joe Kent, combat veteran who resigned over Iran war?Kent, who was nominated by the president and confirmed by the Senate last year, shared his resignation letter on X, objecting to the decision to go to war with Iran despite, as he stated, "no imminent threat" to the United States.

Gurugram man, battling depression, jumps from 23rd floor with 2-year-old daughterRahul, a school operator from Delhi, had been struggling with mental stress and health problems. The police confirmed no suicide note was found, and the bodies were sent for post-mortem before being handed to the family.

Haldi Ghati Award for Teen Taal podcast's Kamlesh SinghThe national award, presented by Lakshyaraj Singh Mewar, the Managing Trustee of the Maharana of Mewar Charitable Foundation (MMCF), recognises Kamlesh Singh's contributions of three decades to Indian media.

30 years later, deja vu for Rajinikanth as out-of-politics Thalaiva is dragged into politicsThirty years ago, the Superstar owned the 1996 election with his swag, style and one-liner. He had famously said, ''Even God cannot save Tamil Nadu if the AIADMK returns to power."

Why Iran is hitting UAE the hardestThe UAE has faced the most intense barrage of Iranian attacks among the Gulf countries since the US-Israel war on Tehran began. Civilian and business infrastructure in Dubai and other hubs have been targeted. But why has the UAE been targeted more than its Gulf neighbours? Here's what's driving Iran's aggression against it.

Did Kamran Akmal call Mohsin Naqvi a trophy chor after Bangladesh series loss?Former Pakistan wicketkeeper Kamran Akmal slammed the team's poor performance in the series against Bangladesh, calling Pakistan cricket a comedy series. His comments also indirectly referenced PCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi, linked to the Asia Cup controversy.

Live: Projectile hits near atomic power plant in Iran, says state mediaUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict continues to escalate with fresh flashpoints across the region. Iran has linked disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz directly to the ongoing war and rejected separating maritime security from the conflict, even as countries like Greece push for a long-term solution to keep the crucial shipping lane open. India has denied any vessel-swap talks with Iran but said it remains engaged to bring back stranded ships, while the UAE briefly shut its airspace amid missile and drone threats before resuming operations. On the ground, Israeli strikes have intensified across Lebanon with casualties reported among Lebanese soldiers, as Tehran steps up internal crackdowns over alleged foreign links. Meanwhile, the White House signalled the conflict may be short-lived, with US officials asserting the American economy remains resilient. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest live updates.

Will continue to strike Iran: Israel's first reaction on Larijani's killingIsrael's envoy to India, Reuven Azhar, said Israel will continue its military campaign against Iran, defending strikes that killed senior figures like Ali Larijani. He claimed Iran's military capabilities have been significantly degraded and warned there will be no de-escalation unless Tehran changes its policies or leadership.

Did your health insurance premium jump at renewal? Here's what's behind itMany people are seeing sudden jumps in their health insurance premiums at renewal. The reasons aren't always clear, and insurers don't always explain them. In this article, we break down the possible reasons behind these sudden premium jumps.

Nato's 'nay' to Trump: Why Europe wants no part of America's war on IranThe conflict initiated by Donald Trump has escalated into a difficult war with Iran, but European Nato allies have largely refused to support it, arguing it is not their responsibility and prioritising their own security concerns. Europe's reluctance stems from lack of consultation, domestic opposition, and fear of weakening defenses against Russia, while instead pushing for a diplomatic resolution.

Menstrual leave debate returns: Why women must hide biology to succeedThe debate around menstrual leave has resurfaced after the Supreme Court said mandating it may discourage companies from hiring women. But the controversy raises a deeper question: why are women still expected to ignore biological realities and work within systems designed almost entirely around male physiology?

When Nasa told Kalpana Chawla in space: The Indian PM is on the lineKalpana Chawla's historic journey began on November 19, 1997, when she flew aboard the STS-87 Columbia mission as part of a six-member crew.

sports.headline: Emma Raducanu pulls out of Miami Open, continues struggling in challenging season British tennis star and 2021 US Open champion Emma Raducanu has withdrawn from the 2026 Miami Open due to illness and lingering post-viral symptoms, missing a chance to defend her 215 ranking points.

Did Kamran Akmal call Mohsin Naqvi a trophy chor after Bangladesh series loss? Former Pakistan wicketkeeper Kamran Akmal slammed the team's poor performance in the series against Bangladesh, calling Pakistan cricket a comedy series. His comments also indirectly referenced PCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi, linked to the Asia Cup controversy.

Pratika Rawal recalls emotional ride in Women's World Cup final: Didn't see last ball Pratika Rawal described the Women's World Cup final against South Africa as an emotional whirlwind, saying she didn't even watch the last ball. The right-handed batter missed the final due to an ankle and knee injury.

Mexico willing to host Iran's matches, after team refuses to play in USA Mexico is willing to host Iran's FIFA World Cup 2026 matches after the West Asian nation expressed reluctance to play in the United States amid its ongoing conflict involving Israel. The Mexican President said the country would have no problem hosting the games.

No more impact sub, but Rohit might spend time on the bench, hints MI coach IPL: Senior Mumbai Indians batter Rohit Sharma will not be used as an impact substitute this season, head coach Mahela Jayawardene said during a media interaction on March 17.

Australia cricketers in doubt for PSL 2026? PCB opens up on security concerns report The PCB has dismissed concerns over overseas players' travel for Pakistan Super League 2026, insisting all arrangements are in place and Australian players who have signed up will begin arriving next week.

Pak-origin cricketer hits back at Gavaskar after Abrar Ahmed's signing in The Hundred Azeem Rafiq hits out at Sunil Gavaskar over his remarks on Abrar Ahmed's Hundred signing, calling them "ridiculous" as the controversy around IPL-linked ownership and Pakistan players grows.

MCC criticises Salman Agha for bizarre run out against Bangladesh, backs Mehidy Hasan MCC clarified that Salman Agha's run-out in the second ODI against Bangladesh in Mirpur was fully valid under the Laws, stating he was clearly out of his crease while the ball remained in play.

R Ashwin questions fan army agendas, slams superhero culture in Indian cricket R Ashwin slammed the growing influence of fan armies and superhero culture in Indian cricket, saying the game is increasingly being overshadowed by PR-driven narratives, brand-building, and personality-focused debates.

Conway sparks carnage as New Zealand crush South Africa to win 2nd T20I by 68 runs Devon Conway's 60 and a fiery pace display from Ben Sears and Lockie Ferguson helped New Zealand crush South Africa by 68 runs, levelling the T20I series after a dramatic collapse.

Sensational Amelia Kerr scripts history with 100 Women's T20I wickets NZW vs SAW: New Zealand skipper Amelia Kerr became the fastest cricketer from a full-member nation to complete 1,000 runs and 100 wickets in Women's T20Is, reaching the milestone in her 94th match against South Africa.

Experience then, fiery passion now: Suryakumar Yadav on India's evolution in T20IsT20 World Cup: Suryakumar Yadav compares India's 2024 and 2026 T20 World Cup wins, highlighting the shift from experienced composure to youthful intensity and how home pressure shaped the title defence.

KKR handed major scare as Harshit Rana set to miss most of IPL 2026IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders have been handed a big injury scare. Fast bowler Harshit Rana, who was set to lead the team's attack, is most likely to miss most of the tournament. According to reports, Rana is not going to recover in time from his knee injury.

IPL 2026 injury worries grow: Cummins, Hazlewood doubtful for opening gamesSunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins and RCB pacer Josh Hazlewood are doubtful for the opening games of IPL 2026. Both Australian quicks are still awaiting medical clearance.

FIFA World Cup to suffer same fate as cricket? Iran urges games to be moved out of USAIran's football federation is pushing to move its 2026 World Cup matches from the US to Mexico over safety fears, in a controversy drawing comparisons to Bangladesh's T20 World Cup crisis.

Tilak Varma felicitated by Telangana CM Revanth Reddy after T20 World Cup triumphIndia T20 World Cup winner Tilak Varma received a warm welcome in his home state after the tournament triumph. Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy felicitated the young batter for his role in India's historic title defence.

Is CSK forcing a spot for MS Dhoni? De Villiers puzzled by veteran's role in IPL 2026IPL 2026: AB de Villiers questions MS Dhoni's role at CSK in IPL 2026, saying the legend must bat higher or risk playing for the wrong reasons as Sanju Samson's arrival reshapes Chennai's lineup.

IPL as big as international cricket: Ricky Ponting explains India's huge talent poolRicky Ponting believes the Indian Premier League plays a major role in shaping India's T20 success. The former Australia captain said IPL matches are as intense as international games and help prepare players for the national team.

Neymar focused on FIFA World Cup dream despite another Brazil squad snubNeymar's hopes of playing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup remain alive despite another Brazil squad snub. The Santos forward admitted he was disappointed after being left out of Brazil's upcoming friendlies in the United States.

Watch: Rohit Sharma in top gear in MI nets session ahead of IPL 2026IPL 2026: Rohit Sharma has already started turning heads ahead of IPL 2026. A viral video from Mumbai Indians' training session showed the former India captain going after the bowlers, raising excitement among fans before the new season.

A war crime: Afghanistan cricketers slam deadly Pak strikes on Kabul hospitalAfghanistan cricket stars Rashid Khan and Mohammad Nabi have strongly condemned reported Pakistani airstrikes in Kabul. The two players expressed grief and anger over civilian casualties after a hospital was reportedly struck during the attack.

KKR's fast bowling problem: 2024 winners head towards shaky start to IPL campaignKolkata Knight Riders enter the new IPL season with a fast-bowling unit already battered by injuries and uncertainty. With Harshit Rana unlikely to feature and Matheesha Pathirana still recovering, the defending champions suddenly look short of pace options and experience.

Pratika Rawal opens up on her deep bond with Test cricket: This is my formatPratika Rawal said that she considers Test cricket the ultimate measure of a cricketer's character, patience, and determination. She made her debut in the Day-Night Perth Test against Australia, scoring her maiden fifty.

Chennai Super Kings appoint ex-England wicketkeeper as fielding coach for IPL 2026Chennai Super Kings have appointed former England wicketkeeper-batter James Foster as their new fielding coach ahead of IPL 2026, adding an experienced coach to Stephen Fleming's support staff.

Liam Livingstone slams Brendon McCullum, Rob Key in sensational rant against ECBLiam Livingstone launched a blistering attack on England's team management, accusing Brendon McCullum and Rob Key of poor communication and a lack of support after being dropped from the white-ball setup.

The story of how Suryakumar, not Hardik Pandya, became India's T20I captainSuryakumar Yadav was made the T20I captain after the 2024 World Cup, pipping the then vice-captain Hardik Pandya. The batter reveals how he got to know about the news.

Vaishnavi Adkar continues dream run, closes in on India's No.1 WTA rankingVaishnavi Adkar's rapid rise continued as she broke into the WTA top 400 for the first time, climbing to a career-best No. 397 after winning the ITF W35 Kalaburagi title and moving within six spots of India's No.1 ranking.

Pakistan fast bowler grabs limelight with massive no-ball in National T20 CupPakistan pacer Asad Akhtar grabbed attention after bowling a huge no-ball during Karachi Blues' National T20 Cup clash against Lahore Blues on Saturday at the Imran Khan Cricket Stadium in Peshawar.

Chelsea fined 131 crore rupees, 1-year-ban for breaching financial rulesChelsea have been fined $14.3 million and handed a suspended transfer ban after the Premier League found the club guilty of breaching financial regulations. Undisclosed payments, made between 2011 and 2018 have landed the club in trouble.

It is not Shubman Gill: Fans slam BCCI over huge mistake in Naman AwardsThe Indian Cricket Board is being called out for an extremely silly mistake at the Naman Awards 2026. During the ceremony, BCCI credited Shubman Gill as the captain who won the Champions Trophy, and not Rohit Sharma.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
